title: GC Evidence Bundle
description: Signed machine-readable evidence record produced by dxp-init audit operations. Consumable by auditors, automated compliance platforms, and GC-compatible tooling. Aligns with gc-compliance-evidence.schema.json (GC), SLSA provenance v1.0 predicate shape, JOSE EdDSA/Ed25519 signing, and SBOM reference formats.

bundle_id.description: UUID v4 — unique per audit run

operation.$comment: EU AI Act Article 12 relevance: records the audited/install/status operation as an event class for traceability over the system lifecycle.

principal_ref.$comment: EU AI Act Article 13 relevance: supports provider/operator identity traceability when evidence is presented to deployers or auditors.
principal_ref.description: principal_id from gc-principal.yaml. Full principal record is not embedded to avoid bloat; verify via the signing public key.

principal_snapshot.$comment: EU AI Act Article 13 relevance: carries the minimal actor/provider context needed to interpret who produced the evidence bundle.
principal_snapshot.description: Minimal snapshot of principal fields at time of run for standalone verification

timestamp.$comment: EU AI Act Article 12 relevance: supports log chronology and audit traceability for the audited event.

iso.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of run completion

time_loc.description: time.loc v1.2 canonical temporal-spatial primitive (see time-loc/ project)

epoch_ms.description: Unix epoch milliseconds for deterministic sorting

validator.$comment: EU AI Act Article 9 relevance: identifies the control/check used in the risk management and review process; Article 13 relevance: explains how evidence was produced.

version.description: Semver of the dxp-init tool that produced this bundle

scope.$comment: EU AI Act Article 12 relevance: identifies the system/artifacts covered by the log record; Article 9 relevance: bounds the risk-management evidence to a project and file set.

root.description: Absolute path to the scanned directory

files_scanned.description: Relative paths of all files included in this audit

preset.description: Named preset applied, if any (e.g. soc2, nist-ai-rmf, eu-ai-act-readiness)

findings.$comment: EU AI Act Article 9 relevance: records identified risks, control failures, remediations, and residual conformance state for iterative risk management.

summary.$comment: EU AI Act Article 9 relevance: summarizes risk/control outcome and closure state; Article 13 relevance: makes the result understandable without replaying the full audit.

conformant.description: True only when errors === 0 and all required checks passed

gee_envelope.$comment: EU AI Act Article 12 relevance: optional governed event wrapper for interoperable record-keeping; should include time.loc in the full GEE record.
gee_envelope.description: Optional Governed Event Envelope wrapper. Present when agentic governance is enabled in gc-principal.yaml.

slsa_provenance.$comment: EU AI Act Article 12 relevance: provenance record for how this evidence artifact was produced; Article 9 relevance: supports supply-chain risk controls for the evidence-generation process.
slsa_provenance.description: Optional in-toto Statement carrying a SLSA provenance v1.0 predicate. This records how the evidence bundle artifact was produced; it is not a substitute for signing the bundle.

buildType.description: SLSA Build Type v1.0 URI identifying the parameterized template/process used to produce this bundle.

externalParameters.description: Tenant/user-controlled parameters to the evidence-generation run; downstream verifiers treat these as untrusted inputs.

internalParameters.description: Builder-controlled parameters useful for reproducibility, debugging, or incident response.

id.description: URI for the trusted build/evidence-generation platform.

sbom_ref.$comment: EU AI Act Article 9 relevance: supports supply-chain risk management by linking this evidence bundle to an SBOM. Recommendation: use CycloneDX JSON for dxp-init's first implementation because CycloneDX has a broad supply-chain transparency object model and registered JSON media type; allow SPDX because it is an ISO standard and common in procurement workflows.
sbom_ref.description: Optional SBOM reference. Prefer CycloneDX JSON for first implementation; SPDX is also allowed for interoperability and procurement contexts.

version.description: SBOM format version, e.g. CycloneDX 1.7 or SPDX 3.0.

uri.description: Relative or absolute URI to the SBOM document.

signature.$comment: EU AI Act Article 12 relevance: supports integrity of the log/evidence record. RFC 8037 alignment: JWS protected header alg=EdDSA; verification key is JWK kty=OKP, crv=Ed25519.
signature.description: JWS compact serialization using JOSE EdDSA over Ed25519 per RFC 8037. Signing input is the canonical bundle with this signature field excluded. Verifiable using the principal public JWK.
